phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: E a caster minion and let the visions farm for you — your lane is a macro pattern, not a duel, from the first wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q online: the silence is your trade answer. Use it on his engage spell, not on poke he can walk around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your voidling-silence suppression beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — voidling-silence suppression edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Nether Grasp online — with ANY jungle presence you now have a guaranteed kill button. The lane bends around your 6.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your voidling-silence suppression in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your voidling-silence suppression on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your voidling-silence suppression beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q online: the silence is your trade answer. Use it on his engage spell, not on poke he can walk around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W voidlings turn your shove permanent — the wave hits his tower while you ward, roam-shadow, or just exist safely.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your voidling-silence suppression out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Nether Grasp online — with ANY jungle presence you now have a guaranteed kill button. The lane bends around your 6.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight edge on first item: your voidling-silence suppression beat Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Malz is a number: one suppression on their carry equals one won teamfight. Shield up, flank smart, press R.

phases.why: Late Malz is a number: one suppression on their carry equals one won teamfight. Shield up, flank smart, press R.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: E a caster minion and let the visions farm for you — your lane is a macro pattern, not a duel, from the first wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Q online: the silence is your trade answer. Use it on his engage spell, not on poke he can walk around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W voidlings turn your shove permanent — the wave hits his tower while you ward, roam-shadow, or just exist safely.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter and the machine is complete: E-hops fund themselves, the wave is always crashing, and his tower is always bleeding.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Nether Grasp online — with ANY jungle presence you now have a guaranteed kill button. The lane bends around your 6.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Liandry's spike: visions melt waves AND health bars. Suppress-into-burn kills through most defensive answers.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items your voidling-silence suppression beat the point-click spear bullying in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Pantheon answer you, not the other way around.
